 {"id":"rich_rust-4ot","title":"Set Up Golden File Test Infrastructure","description":"Create golden file (snapshot) testing infrastructure for visual regression detection.

PURPOSE:
Golden file tests capture expected rendering output and detect visual regressions by comparing actual output against stored expectations. This is CRITICAL for a terminal rendering library.

INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIREMENTS:

1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE:
   tests/golden/
   - fixtures/           (Input data for tests)
   - expected/           (Golden output files)
   - golden_test.rs      (Test runner)

2. TEST RUNNER FEATURES:
   - Compare actual vs expected output
   - Strip ANSI codes option for text-only comparison
   - Generate diff on failure
   - Auto-update mode (GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 env var)
   - Detailed failure messages with line-by-line diff

3. REQUIRED GOLDEN FILES (minimum):
   - table_basic.golden
   - table_styled.golden
   - panel_simple.golden
   - panel_nested.golden
   - text_wrapped.golden
   - rule_variants.golden
   - tree_nested.golden
   - columns_layout.golden

4. IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS:
   - Use insta crate for snapshot testing
   - OR custom implementation with file comparison

5. LOGGING:
   - Use tracing on comparison functions
   - Log: test name, expected path, lengths
   - On failure: log diff location

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Golden file infrastructure compiles and runs
- At least 8 golden files created
- Auto-update mode works
- Failures produce readable diffs
- CI integration documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:43.197631197-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:14:04.764355724-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:14:04.764355724-05:00","close_reason":"Golden file test infrastructure complete: created tests/golden_test.rs with 31 snapshot tests covering Rule (4), Panel (6), Table (7), Tree (3), Columns (2), ProgressBar (4), and combined/edge-case tests (5). Generated 31 snapshot files in tests/snapshots/. All 37 tests pass.","labels":["critical","infrastructure","testing"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-4s3","title":"Validate Measurement Protocol against RICH_SPEC Section 5","description":"## Objective
Verify the Measurement implementation in src/measure.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 5 exactly.

## Background
The Measurement protocol determines how much horizontal space a renderable needs. It returns minimum and maximum widths, enabling the layout system to calculate optimal column widths for tables and panels.

## Validation Checklist

### 5.1 Measurement Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: minimum (usize), maximum (usize)
- [ ] Verify invariant: minimum <= maximum always maintained
- [ ] Verify Measurement::new(minimum, maximum) constructor
- [ ] Verify constructor normalizes if minimum > maximum

### 5.2 Measurement Methods
- [ ] Verify normalize() ensures minimum <= maximum
- [ ] Verify with_maximum(width) clamps both values
- [ ] Verify with_minimum(width) sets floor for both values
- [ ] Verify clamp(min, max) constrains to range
- [ ] Verify union(other) takes max of maximums, min of minimums
- [ ] Verify span accessor returns maximum - minimum

### 5.3 Utility Functions
- [ ] Verify measure_union(measurements) unions all measurements
- [ ] Verify measure_sum(measurements) sums all measurements
- [ ] Verify get_measurement returns Measurement for any Measurable

### 5.4 Measurable Trait (if implemented)
- [ ] Verify trait signature: fn measure(&self, max_width: usize) -> Measurement
- [ ] Verify implementations for: Text, Segment, Table, Panel

### 5.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test measurement with zero width content
- [ ] Test measurement with very large content
- [ ] Test clamp with inverted min/max

## Files to Examine
- src/measure.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 5","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Invariant maintenance confirmed.","notes":"Validation complete: Measurement struct, Measurement::get, and measure_renderables match RICH_SPEC Section 5; no code changes needed. Blocked only by epic rich_rust-q1k closure.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.545908076-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T21:44:10.464454021-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-4s3","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.860321866-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-4sr","title":"Validate Text Operations against RICH_SPEC Section 6","description":"## Objective
Verify the Text implementation in src/text.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 6 exactly.

## Background
Text is the primary user-facing type for styled text. It supports overlapping style spans, wrapping, justification, and conversion to segments. This is one of the most complex modules.

## Validation Checklist

### 6.1 Text Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: plain (String), spans (Vec<Span>), style (Option<Style>), justify (JustifyMethod), overflow (OverflowMethod), tab_size (usize), no_wrap (bool)
- [ ] Verify Span struct: start (usize), end (usize), style (Style)
- [ ] Verify JustifyMethod enum: Default, Left, Center, Right, Full
- [ ] Verify OverflowMethod enum: Fold, Crop, Ellipsis

### 6.2 Text Construction
- [ ] Verify Text::new() creates empty text
- [ ] Verify Text::plain(text) creates unstyled text
- [ ] Verify Text::styled(text, style) creates styled text
- [ ] Verify From<String> and From<&str> implementations

### 6.3 Text Manipulation
- [ ] Verify append(text) adds plain text
- [ ] Verify append_styled(text, style) adds styled text
- [ ] Verify stylize(start, end, style) applies span
- [ ] Verify highlight_words(words, style) highlights matches
- [ ] Verify highlight_regex(pattern, style) highlights regex matches

### 6.4 Text Rendering
- [ ] Verify render() returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify spans are applied in order with proper overlap handling
- [ ] Verify base style is applied before spans
- [ ] Verify tab characters are expanded to tab_size spaces

### 6.5 Text Wrapping (CRITICAL - complex algorithm)
- [ ] Verify wrap(width) returns Vec<Text> lines
- [ ] Verify word boundaries are respected
- [ ] Verify long words are broken at width
- [ ] Verify overflow methods: Fold (break anywhere), Crop (truncate), Ellipsis (truncate with ...)
- [ ] Verify no_wrap flag prevents wrapping

### 6.6 Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Verify divide(offsets) splits text at character positions
- [ ] Verify spans are correctly split across divisions
- [ ] Verify partial spans are properly trimmed
- [ ] Test: divide([5,10]) on 'hello world!' with span on 'world'

### 6.7 Justification
- [ ] Verify left justify (no change)
- [ ] Verify right justify (pad left)
- [ ] Verify center justify (pad both sides)
- [ ] Verify full justify (expand spaces between words)

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 6","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Text.divide() algorithm matches spec exactly. Wrapping edge cases handled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:58.566928519-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:58.566928519-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-4sr","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:36.985802031-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-5vo","title":"Validate Panel against RICH_SPEC Section 10","description":"## Objective
Verify the Panel implementation in src/renderables/panel.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 10 exactly.

## Background
Panel draws a bordered box around content with optional title and subtitle. It uses the Box system for borders and supports content alignment.

## Validation Checklist

### 10.1 Panel Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: content, title, subtitle, box_style, border_style, title_align, subtitle_align, padding, expand, width, height, highlight, safe_box

### 10.2 Panel Construction
- [ ] Verify Panel::new(content) constructor
- [ ] Verify builder methods: title(), subtitle(), box_style(), etc.
- [ ] Verify Panel::fit(content) creates minimal-width panel

### 10.3 Panel Rendering
- [ ] Verify top border with title integration
- [ ] Verify content area with padding
- [ ] Verify bottom border with subtitle integration
- [ ] Verify title alignment: left, center, right

### 10.4 Panel Sizing
- [ ] Verify width calculation includes borders and padding
- [ ] Verify expand=true fills available width
- [ ] Verify explicit width overrides
- [ ] Verify height expands to fit content

### 10.5 Integration
- [ ] Test Panel containing Text
- [ ] Test Panel containing Table
- [ ] Test nested Panels

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/panel.rs (primary)
- src/box.rs (BoxChars)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 10","acceptance_criteria":"Panel renders correctly with all border styles and alignments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:39.461862209-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:40:02.662601098-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:40:02.662601098-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Panel

### Summary
The Panel implementation in src/renderables/panel.rs is **MOSTLY ALIGNED** with RICH_SPEC.md Section 10.3-10.4. Core rendering logic matches spec.

---

### 10.3 Panel Struct Fields

| Field | Spec | Impl | Status |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| content | Box<dyn Renderable> | Vec<Vec<Segment>> | ⚠️ Simplified |
| box_style | Box | &'static BoxChars | ✅ |
| safe_box | Option<bool> | bool | ✅ (simplified) |
| expand | bool | bool | ✅ |
| style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| border_style | Style | Style | ✅ |
| width | Option<usize> | Option<usize> | ✅ |
| height | Option<usize> | Option<usize> | ✅ |
| padding | PaddingDimensions | PaddingDimensions | ✅ |
| highlight | bool | - | ❌ Missing |
| title | Option<Text> | Option<Text> | ✅ |
| title_align | JustifyMethod | JustifyMethod | ✅ |
| subtitle | Option<Text> | Option<Text> | ✅ |
| subtitle_align | JustifyMethod | JustifyMethod | ✅ |

---

### 10.4 Rendering Structure - ALIGNED

| Step | Spec | Impl | Status |
|------|------|------|--------|
| Safe box handling | ✅ | effective_box() | ✅ |
| Width calculation | expand/width/measure | expand/width/content_width() | ✅ |
| Top border + title | insert_title_into_border | render_top_border() | ✅ |
| Top padding | loop with blank lines | loop with spaces | ✅ |
| Content with borders | head[0] + content + head[3] | Same | ✅ |
| Bottom padding | loop with blank lines | loop with spaces | ✅ |
| Bottom border + subtitle | Similar to top | render_bottom_border() | ✅ |

---

### Constructors & Methods - ALIGNED

- Panel::new(content_lines) ✅
- Panel::from_text(str) ✅
- Panel::from_rich_text(text, width) ✅
- fit_panel(text) ✅ (global function)
- Builder methods: title(), subtitle(), box_style(), padding(), etc. ✅

---

### Key Differences

1. **Content Storage**: Impl stores pre-rendered Vec<Vec<Segment>> vs spec's Box<dyn Renderable>. This simplifies rendering but prevents dynamic re-layout.

2. **highlight field**: Not implemented (used for background highlighting).

3. **fit()**: Implemented as global fit_panel() function.

---

### Tests - 9 unit tests covering core functionality

### Overall: PASS (with minor simplifications)","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-5vo","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.193170407-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-60o","title":"Verify Exclusions Match RICH_SPEC Section 15","description":"## Objective
Confirm that excluded features from RICH_SPEC.md Section 15 are NOT implemented, avoiding scope creep.

## Background
The spec explicitly lists features excluded from the Rust port. This task verifies we haven't accidentally implemented excluded features and documents any intentional deviations.

## Validation Checklist

### 15.1 Explicitly Excluded Features
- [ ] Verify NO Live display (auto-updating regions)
- [ ] Verify NO Status (spinner status lines)  
- [ ] Verify NO Logging integration
- [ ] Verify NO Pretty printing of Python objects
- [ ] Verify NO Traceback formatting
- [ ] Verify NO Inspect (object introspection)
- [ ] Verify NO Prompt toolkit integration

### 15.2 Partially Excluded Features
- [ ] Verify Console.record is basic or absent
- [ ] Verify no HTML/SVG export (unless explicitly added)
- [ ] Verify no ANSI art rendering

### 15.3 Implementation Deviations (Documented)
- [ ] Document any intentionally added features not in Python Rich
- [ ] Document any Rust-specific features (e.g., thread safety)

### 15.4 Future Considerations
- [ ] List features that COULD be added later but aren't now
- [ ] Ensure clear boundary between Phase 1-4 and future work

## Files to Examine
- All source files (absence check)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 15
- PLAN_TO_PORT_RICH_TO_RUST.md (scope)","acceptance_criteria":"All exclusions verified. No scope creep detected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:14.984399045-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:14.984399045-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-60o","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.608428343-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-62c","title":"Create E2E Test: Complete Rendering Pipeline","description":"## Objective
Create end-to-end tests that verify the complete rendering pipeline from markup to ANSI output.

## Background
The rendering pipeline is: markup string → parse → styled segments → ANSI codes → terminal output. E2E tests verify this entire chain works correctly.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Markup Rendering
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_basic_markup() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info\!("Starting E2E basic markup test");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(80).color_system(ColorSystem::TrueColor);
    let mut output = Vec::new();
    
    info\!(input = "[bold red]Hello[/] [green]World[/]\!", "Rendering markup");
    console.render_to(&mut output, "[bold red]Hello[/] [green]World[/]\!");
    
    let result = String::from_utf8(output).unwrap();
    debug\!(output = %result, "Raw ANSI output");
    
    // Verify ANSI codes present
    assert\!(result.contains("\x1b[1m"), "Missing bold code");  // bold
    assert\!(result.contains("\x1b[31m") || result.contains("\x1b[38;2;"), "Missing red");
    assert\!(result.contains("Hello"), "Missing text content");
    
    info\!("E2E basic markup test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Nested Styles
- Test: `[bold][red][underline]text[/][/][/]`
- Verify: All three styles applied, proper reset sequence

### Scenario 3: Style Override
- Test: `[red]a[blue]b[/]c[/]`
- Verify: 'a' is red, 'b' is blue, 'c' is red again

### Scenario 4: Color System Downgrade
- Test same markup with TrueColor, EightBit, Standard
- Verify: Output is valid for each system

### Scenario 5: Wide Characters
- Test: `[bold]你好世界[/]`
- Verify: Correct cell width calculation

### Scenario 6: Hyperlinks
- Test: `[link=https://example.com]Click[/]`
- Verify: OSC 8 sequences present

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. Test name and scenario at INFO level
2. Input data at DEBUG level
3. Intermediate state (parsed segments) at TRACE level
4. Output data at DEBUG level
5. Assertion results at INFO level
6. Timing for each phase

## Golden File Comparison
```rust
fn assert_golden(name: &str, actual: &str) {
    let golden_path = format\!("tests/golden/{}.txt", name);
    if std::env::var("UPDATE_GOLDEN").is_ok() {
        std::fs::write(&golden_path, actual).unwrap();
        info\!(path = %golden_path, "Updated golden file");
    } else {
        let expected = std::fs::read_to_string(&golden_path).unwrap();
        if actual \!= expected {
            error\!(expected = %expected, actual = %actual, "Golden file mismatch");
            panic\!("Output doesn't match golden file");
        }
        info\!("Golden file comparison PASSED");
    }
}
```

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_rendering.rs
- tests/golden/*.txt (golden files)","acceptance_criteria":"All pipeline scenarios pass. Golden files established. Logs show full trace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:28.537406796-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:25:03.535019652-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:25:03.535019652-05:00","close_reason":"E2E rendering pipeline tests complete: created tests/e2e_rendering.rs with 18 tests (plus 8 common test utilities) covering basic markup (3), nested styles (2), style override (1), color system downgrade (3), wide characters (2), hyperlinks (1), edge cases (3), and snapshot tests (2). All 26 tests pass.","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-62c","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.834667593-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-62c","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:53.963675025-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-q1k","title":"EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment","description":"# EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment

## Overview
Comprehensive validation of the Rust implementation against RICH_SPEC.md to ensure behavioral parity with Python Rich library.

## Background & Reasoning
The rich_rust project is a port of Python's Rich library to Rust. The RICH_SPEC.md document contains the complete behavioral specification extracted from Python Rich source (v13.x). This epic ensures:

1. **Behavioral Correctness**: Every feature works exactly as specified
2. **Edge Case Coverage**: Boundary conditions handled correctly  
3. **Algorithm Fidelity**: Complex algorithms (color conversion, ratio distribution, text division) match spec
4. **API Completeness**: All documented methods and properties exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All spec sections validated against implementation
- [ ] Discrepancies documented and resolved
- [ ] Test coverage for each validated behavior
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

## Child Tasks (by module)
1. Color System validation
2. Style System validation
3. Segment operations validation
4. Markup parser validation
5. Measurement protocol validation
6. Text object validation (CRITICAL: division algorithm)
7. Console validation
8. Box characters validation
9. Table layout validation (CRITICAL: width calculation)
10. Panel & Padding validation
11. Alignment system validation
12. Unicode cell width validation
13. Text wrapping validation
14. Ratio distribution validation

## Dependencies
- Blocks: All implementation refinement tasks
- Blocks: All performance optimization tasks

## Technical Notes
- Use the RICH_SPEC.md as the source of truth
- Create property-based tests where applicable
- Document any intentional deviations from spec","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:29.484048364-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:25:35.812552966-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths
  Input:  widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  Expect: [30,40,30]  (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)

Test 2: collapse_widths
  Input:  widths=[40,30,30], minimums=[10,10,10], available=70
  Expect: ~[27,21,22] (excess 30 reduced proportionally)

Test 3: boundary
  Input:  widths=[10,10,10], minimums=[10,10,10], available=20
  Expect: [10,10,10] (can't go below minimum, clipped)
```

## Logging Requirements
When running tests, log:
- Input widths, minimums, maximums
- Available width
- Each step of the algorithm
- Final output widths
- Verification that sum matches

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (width calculation functions)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"Width algorithms match spec EXACTLY. All test vectors pass. Logs show calculation steps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:41.664745764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete

### Findings Summary

1. **calculate_widths()** - CORRECT: Main algorithm follows spec structure

2. **expand_widths()** - CRITICAL DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-t54):
   - Distributes extra space equally instead of by column ratio
   - Column.ratio field is completely ignored
   
3. **collapse_widths()** - MINOR DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-gk4):
   - Missing rounding error correction loop from spec
   - May cause off-by-one in edge cases

### Deliverables
- Created tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs with test vectors
- Created bug issues: rich_rust-t54 (P1), rich_rust-gk4 (P2)
- All tests pass

### Test Vector Evidence
- expand_widths: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  - Expected: [30,40,30], Actual: [33,33,34]","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-qgs","title":"Validate Markup Parser against RICH_SPEC Section 4","description":"## Objective
Verify the Markup parser in src/markup/mod.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 4 exactly.

## Background
The markup parser converts Rich-style markup like '[bold red]Hello[/]' into styled segments. It's the primary user-facing API for styling text. The parser handles nested tags, escaping, and error recovery.

## Validation Checklist

### 4.1 Tag Syntax
- [ ] Verify opening tag: [style] where style is parsed by Style::parse
- [ ] Verify closing tag: [/] closes most recent tag
- [ ] Verify named close: [/bold] closes specific tag
- [ ] Verify escape sequence: backslash-bracket produces literal bracket

### 4.2 ParseElement Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Text(String), Open(Tag), Close(Option<String>)
- [ ] Verify Tag struct contains: name (String), style (Style)

### 4.3 Parser Behavior
- [ ] Verify render(markup) returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify nested tags: [bold][red]text[/][/] applies both styles
- [ ] Verify style inheritance: inner tags combine with outer
- [ ] Verify unmatched close tags are treated as literal text
- [ ] Verify unclosed tags at end are auto-closed

### 4.4 Error Handling (MarkupError)
- [ ] Verify InvalidTag for malformed tags
- [ ] Verify UnmatchedClose for close without open
- [ ] Verify error recovery: invalid markup rendered as literal

### 4.5 Complex Cases
- [ ] Test: [bold]a[italic]b[/italic]c[/bold] - proper nesting
- [ ] Test: [red]a[blue]b[/]c[/] - color override then restore
- [ ] Test: [link=URL]click[/] - hyperlink
- [ ] Test: escaped brackets - literal brackets
- [ ] Test: [bold red on blue]text[/] - compound style

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (primary)
- src/style.rs (Style::parse)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Parser handles all edge cases correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.871978582-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:35:40.042934881-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:35:40.042934881-05:00","close_reason":"Markup Parser validation complete - all RICH_SPEC Section 4 requirements verified:
- Tag syntax: [tag], [/], [/tag], [tag=param], [@handler] supported
- Regex pattern: (\\*)\[([a-z#/@][^\[\]]*?)\] matches spec (improved to exclude both brackets)
- Parsing algorithm: optimization for no '[', style stack, escape handling, auto-close unclosed tags
- Error handling: MarkupError with UnmatchedClosingTag and InvalidTag variants

 {"id":"rich_rust-t54","title":"expand_widths() ignores column ratios, distributes space equally","description":"## Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:612-637

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 14.4)**:
Distribute extra space among columns proportionally based on their `ratio` field. Columns with ratio=0 get no extra space.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation distributes extra space EQUALLY across all columns, completely ignoring the `ratio` field.

**Test Vector**:
- Input: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
- Expected: [30,40,30] (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)
- Actual: [33,33,34] (extra 40 distributed equally)

**Impact**:
The Column.ratio(n) builder method exists and sets the field, but it has no effect on table layout during expansion.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Rewrite expand_widths() to follow Section 14.4 ratio_distribute algorithm.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:24.140591733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:31:20.880305518-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:31:20.880305518-05:00","close_reason":"Verified fixed: expand_widths() now correctly uses num_rational::Ratio for ratio-based distribution. All e2e_width_algorithms tests pass (13/13). Test test_expand_widths_with_ratios confirms 1:2:1 ratio produces [30,40,30] as expected.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-t54","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:39.036581663-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-tkj","title":"Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with Test Artifacts","description":"## Objective
Create CI/CD pipeline that runs all tests and preserves artifacts for debugging.

## Background
CI is essential for catching regressions before merge. The pipeline must run unit tests, E2E tests, and benchmarks, preserving logs and artifacts for debugging failures.

## Implementation Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_LOG: debug
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: cargo test --all-features -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee test-output.log
        
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: cargo test --test '*' -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee e2e-output.log
        
      - name: Upload test logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-logs
          path: |
            test-output.log
            e2e-output.log
          retention-days: 30
          
  benchmarks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench -- --noplot 2>&1 | tee bench-output.log
        
      - name: Upload benchmark results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: benchmark-results
          path: |
            bench-output.log
            target/criterion
          retention-days: 90
          
  coverage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install cargo-tarpaulin
        run: cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
        
      - name: Generate coverage
        run: cargo tarpaulin --out Xml --output-dir coverage
        
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: coverage/cobertura.xml
```

### Artifact Preservation
- Test output logs (30 days)
- Benchmark results (90 days)
- Coverage reports
- Golden file diffs on failure

### Failure Notifications
- GitHub status checks
- Optional: Slack/email on failure

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/benchmarks.yml (optional separate)","acceptance_criteria":"CI runs on every PR. Artifacts preserved. Coverage tracked.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","labels":["ci","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-tkj","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.393233497-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
